activities.title: Hawai'i Keiki Museum
activities.desc: Queens' Marketplace. $10/kid $15/adult, open 9am. Hands-on toddler play, free re-entry within 7 days.

activities.title: Nap at home
activities.desc: Daily hard constraint for toddler.

activities.title: Waikoloa Anchialine Ponds (WAPPA)
activities.desc: Brackish lava pools fed by underground seawater/freshwater. Tiny red shrimp ('ōpae'ula) visible. Short flat walk.

activities.title: King's Trail (Ala Kahakai section)
activities.desc: Ancient Hawaiian coastal trail from A-Bay northward. Flat, toddler-friendly. Ancient fishponds en route.

activities.title: Waikoloa Petroglyph Reserve
activities.desc: Free guided tour from Kings' Shops. Thousands of basalt carvings: humans, birds, canoes, astronomical symbols. Thu–Sun 9:30am.

activities.title: Pool / home base
activities.desc: Early dinner, early bed. Rest before Lavaman race day.

activities.title: Cheer squad at A-Bay
activities.desc: Anaeho'omalu Bay — Lavaman finish line. Live music, beach BBQ. Wife + FILs racing.

activities.title: Stick around A-Bay
activities.desc: Anchialine pond walk, King's Trail, palm fronds, turtles.

activities.title: Pu'ukohola Heiau NHS, Kawaihae
activities.desc: FREE. One of the largest restored heiau in Hawai'i, built 1790–91 by Kamehameha I. Dry-stack lava masonry, no mortar. Stroller-friendly.

activities.title: Spencer Beach
activities.desc: Calm, shaded, toddler-safe. Right next door to Heiau.

activities.title: Manta Ray Night Snorkel
activities.desc: ALREADY BOOKED. Confirm time/pickup separately.

activities.title: Home reading
activities.desc: Books brought from home.

activities.title: Waikoloa Dry Forest walk
activities.desc: Lava field ecosystem succession. ʻAʻā vs pāhoehoe textures, pioneer plant species. Carrier recommended for toddler.

activities.title: Waimea town
activities.desc: Grandma on toddler duty. 30 min north, 2,700ft elevation. Anna Ranch Heritage Center or farmers market. Drive via Kohala Mountain Road.

activities.title: Lava rock sorting craft
activities.desc: Collect ʻaʻā vs pāhoehoe samples from resort grounds. Talk viscosity, eruption style, flow rate.

activities.title: Petroglyph-inspired art
activities.desc: Paper and crayons, make your own 'carvings.' Ancient Hawaiian mark-making meets toddler scribbles.

activities.title: KPC Dinner — Kamuela Provision Company
activities.desc: Hilton Waikoloa Village. Romantic patio overlooking Waiulua Bay. Upscale surf 'n' turf, locally sourced. Grandma on toddler duty.

activities.title: Kona Coffee Farm
activities.desc: Kona Coffee Living History Farm or Mountain Thunder. ~45 min south. Volcanic soil chemistry, 1920s Japanese immigrant farming history.

activities.title: Grandma → Keiki Museum (re-entry)
activities.desc: Free re-entry still valid from arrival day visit.

activities.title: Hapuna Beach
activities.desc: On the drive back. One of the best white-sand beaches on the island, calm water, excellent for toddlers.

activities.title: Puako Petroglyph Field
activities.desc: Mauna Lani / Holoholokai Beach Park. Malama Trail: 1.4 miles through keawe forest, past a lava cave, to open lava field. Bring toddler carrier.

activities.title: Nap at home
activities.desc: Rest before big evening.

activities.title: Mauna Kea Summit Sunset + Stargazing Tour
activities.desc: BOOK IMMEDIATELY. Pickup Queens' Marketplace ~2pm, return ~9:30–10pm. 4WD van, parkas, hot meal, 11" telescope. 13,796ft summit — world's tallest volcano base-to-peak. Grandma on full toddler/bedtime duty.

activities.title: Slow morning
activities.desc: Shave ice on Alii Drive if time allows.

activities.title: Pack + souvenir lava rocks
activities.desc: Resort grounds only — NOT from parks.

bookings.action: Go to maunakea.com NOW — fills fast
Call Hilton Waikoloa or book via OpenTable
Show up Kings' Shops 9:30am — no booking needed
Check availability at kona-coffee-living-history-farm.com or mountainthunder.com
